summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the period (T) of an oscillation?

Period (T) The time for one complete cycle of oscillation. The other options — 'The number of cycles per second', 'The maximum displacement', and 'The speed of the oscillation' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the SI unit of frequency?

SI unit — Hertz (Hz). 1 Hz = 1 cycle per second. The other options — 'Second (s)', 'Radian (rad)', and 'Newton (N)' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is angular frequency?

Angular frequency — ω = 2πf = 2π/T (rad/s). The other options — 'ω = f/2π', 'ω = T/2π', and 'ω = πf' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

If T = 0.5 s, what is f?

The correct answer is '2 Hz'. The other options — '0.5 Hz', '1 Hz', and '4 Hz' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:

SI unit — Hertz (Hz). 1 Hz = 1 cycle per second. The other options — 'Second (s)', 'Radian (rad)', and 'Newton (N)' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is angular frequency

Angular frequency — ω = 2πf = 2π/T (rad/s). The other options — 'ω = f/2π', 'ω = T/2π', and 'ω = πf' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
